core_mechanism: an algebraic (Clifford/geometric-algebra) construction generated by a bilinear form, in which conjugate pairs of dynamical variables are packaged as components of a single graded algebraic object whose evolution is fixed by a first-order (linear) equation of motion, with symmetry constraints entering as auxiliary-field (Lagrange-multiplier) equations
brief_reason: The abstract builds the Clifford algebra from a Hermitian bilinear form and encodes both coordinates and their conjugate momenta into one spinor obeying a first-order equation of motion, with constraints arising as the equations of motion for auxiliary fields enforcing gauge invariances.
